accident.def: An unexpected event that causes damage or injury
ex: There was a car accident on the highway this morning.
She had an accident while riding her bicycle.

achieve.def: To succeed in doing something after trying hard
ex: He achieved his goal of running a marathon.
You can achieve anything if you work hard enough.

activity.def: Something that you do for fun or as part of a plan
ex: Swimming is my favorite outdoor activity.
The school offers many after-class activities.

actually.def: Used to say what is really true
ex: I actually enjoyed the movie more than I expected.
He looks young, but he is actually 40 years old.

adult.def: A person who is fully grown
ex: This film is only for adults.
As an adult, you need to make your own decisions.

advantage.def: Something that helps you or is useful
ex: Speaking two languages is a big advantage.
Living near the office has many advantages.
Traveling alone was a great adventure.
The children love reading adventure stories.

advice.def: An opinion about what someone should do
ex: Can you give me some advice about my job?
She followed her mother's advice and studied harder.

afford.def: To have enough money to buy something
ex: We cannot afford a new car right now.
Can you afford to eat out every day?
She is afraid of spiders.
Don't be afraid to ask questions.

airport.def: A place where planes take off and land
ex: We arrived at the airport two hours early.
The airport was very crowded during the holiday.
The fish was still alive when we caught it.
He feels more alive when he is near the sea.

amount.def: How much of something there is
ex: A large amount of water was wasted.
What amount of sugar do you need for the cake?

ancient.def: Very old, from a long time ago
ex: We visited an ancient temple in Greece.
The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids.

anyway.def: Used to change the subject or add something
ex: It was raining, but we went out anyway.
Anyway, let me tell you what happened next.

apartment.def: A set of rooms to live in, usually in a building
ex: They moved into a new apartment last week.
Her apartment is on the third floor.

apologize.def: To say you are sorry for something
ex: He apologized for being late.
You should apologize to her for what you said.
Can you arrange a meeting for tomorrow?
She arranged the flowers in a vase.

attack.def: To try to hurt someone or something
ex: The dog attacked the stranger.
The army attacked the city at night.

attend.def: To go to an event or a place
ex: Will you attend the meeting tomorrow?
She attends a language course every evening.

attractive.def: Pleasant to look at or interesting
ex: She found him very attractive.
The city center is attractive to tourists.

audience.def: People who watch or listen to a show
ex: The audience clapped loudly after the concert.
The speaker asked the audience a question.

avoid.def: To stay away from something or someone
ex: Try to avoid eating too much sugar.
He avoids driving in heavy traffic.
The weather was awful all week.
I feel awful about forgetting her birthday.
You need to learn the basic rules first.
The hotel room was basic but clean.

beach.def: An area of sand next to the sea
ex: We spent the whole day at the beach.
The children built sandcastles on the beach.

beat.def: To win against someone in a game or fight
ex: Our team beat them 3 to 1.
Nobody can beat her at chess.

behave.def: To act in a particular way
ex: The children behaved very well at dinner.
Why is he behaving so strangely today?

belong.def: To be owned by or be a member of
ex: This book belongs to my sister.
He belongs to a local football club.

bicycle.def: A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pushing pedals
ex: She rides her bicycle to school every day.
I got a new bicycle for my birthday.

bite.def: To cut something with your teeth
ex: The dog tried to bite the mailman.
She bit into the apple hungrily.

blame.def: To say someone is responsible for something bad
ex: Don't blame me for your mistakes.
They blamed the rain for the accident.
The blind man used a stick to walk.
She has been blind since birth.

block.def: To stop something from moving or happening
ex: A fallen tree blocked the road.
He blocked the ball with his hands.
The bomb was found before it exploded.
They dropped bombs on the city during the war.

bone.def: One of the hard parts inside your body
ex: She broke a bone in her arm.
The dog was chewing on a bone.
We crossed the border into France.
The town is near the border with Germany.

borrow.def: To take something that you will give back later
ex: Can I borrow your pen for a moment?
She borrowed three books from the library.

boss.def: The person in charge at work
ex: My boss gave me the day off.
She is a strict but fair boss.

bottle.def: A container for liquids, usually made of glass or plastic
ex: He drank a whole bottle of water.
There is an empty bottle on the table.
The keys were at the bottom of my bag.
There is a shop at the bottom of the hill.
The brave firefighter saved the child.
You were very brave at the dentist today.

breath.def: The air that goes in and out of your lungs
ex: Take a deep breath and relax.
He was out of breath after running.

bridge.def: A structure built over a river or road so people can cross
ex: We walked across the old stone bridge.
The bridge connects the two sides of the city.

bright.def: Full of light or strong in color
ex: The sun was very bright this morning.
She wore a bright red dress to the party.
The river is very broad at this point.
He has a broad knowledge of history.
The sea was calm and beautiful.
Stay calm and don't panic.

camera.def: A device used to take photos or videos
ex: She bought a new camera for her trip.
The security camera recorded everything.

camp.def: A place where people stay in tents
ex: The kids went to summer camp for two weeks.
We set up camp near the lake.

cancel.def: To decide that something will not happen
ex: They cancelled the flight because of the storm.
I had to cancel my dentist appointment.

captain.def: The leader of a team or the person in charge of a ship
ex: He is the captain of the football team.
The captain welcomed the passengers on board.
Please listen carefully to the instructions.
She carefully carried the glass of water.

ceiling.def: The top surface of a room
ex: There is a crack in the ceiling.
The ceiling in this room is very high.

celebrate.def: To do something special for a happy event
ex: We celebrated her birthday with a big party.
How do you celebrate New Year in your country?
This castle was built in the 15th century.
A lot has changed in the last century.

character.def: A person in a book, film, or play
ex: Who is your favorite character in the movie?
The main character is a young doctor.
There is no charge for children under five.
What is the charge for delivery?
This restaurant is good and cheap.
I found a cheap flight to London.

check.def: To look at something to see if it is correct or safe
ex: Please check your answers before submitting.
I need to check my email.

chest.def: The front part of your body between your neck and stomach
ex: He had a pain in his chest.
She held the baby close to her chest.

chicken.def: A bird kept for its meat and eggs
ex: We had chicken for dinner last night.
The farmer has 20 chickens on his farm.

circle.def: A round shape like the letter O
ex: The children sat in a circle on the floor.
Draw a circle in the middle of the page.

climb.def: To go up something using your hands and feet
ex: They climbed the mountain in three hours.
The cat climbed up the tree.

clock.def: A device that shows what time it is
ex: The clock on the wall says it is noon.
I set my alarm clock for 6 a.m.

cloud.def: A white or grey thing in the sky made of tiny water drops
ex: Dark clouds appeared before the rain.
There is not a single cloud in the sky today.

coast.def: The land next to the sea
ex: We drove along the coast for hours.
The town is on the south coast.

collect.def: To bring things together or to get things as a hobby
ex: He collects stamps from different countries.
I will collect the children from school at three.

comfortable.def: Making you feel relaxed and at ease
ex: This sofa is very comfortable.
Are you comfortable in that chair?

compare.def: To look at two things to see how they are different or similar
ex: Compare these two pictures and find the differences.
You cannot compare a city with a village.

compete.def: To try to win against others
ex: Five teams will compete in the final.
She competed in the swimming race.

complain.def: To say you are unhappy about something
ex: He always complains about the food.
If the service is bad, you should complain to the manager.
She completed the course in six months.
Please complete this form and sign it.

condition.def: The state that something or someone is in
ex: The car is in excellent condition.
He has a heart condition.
She is confident about passing the exam.
He became more confident after the training.

confirm.def: To say that something is definitely true or will happen
ex: Can you confirm your reservation?
The doctor confirmed that he was healthy.
Connect the printer to your computer.
This road connects the two villages.
This box contains old photos.
The drink contains a lot of sugar.

control.def: To have power over something or make it do what you want
ex: She cannot control her anger sometimes.
The pilot controlled the plane during the storm.

conversation.def: A talk between two or more people
ex: We had a long conversation about our future.
She started a conversation with the man next to her.
All your answers are correct.
Is this the correct address?

couple.def: Two people who are together, or a small number
ex: The couple sat together at the restaurant.
I will be ready in a couple of minutes.

crash.def: To hit something hard, causing damage
ex: The car crashed into a tree.
My computer crashed and I lost all my work.
That was a crazy idea but it worked!
He is crazy about football.
She created a beautiful painting.
The company created 500 new jobs.
Crime is a serious problem in big cities.
He was sent to prison for his crimes.

crowd.def: A large group of people in one place
ex: A crowd gathered outside the stadium.
I don't like shopping in crowds.

cruel.def: Causing pain or suffering to others on purpose
ex: It is cruel to hurt animals.
The villain in the story is very cruel.

culture.def: The beliefs, way of life, and art of a group of people
ex: I want to learn about Japanese culture.
Music is an important part of our culture.

curious.def: Wanting to know or learn about something
ex: Children are naturally curious about the world.
I am curious about what happened.

customer.def: A person who buys something from a shop or business
ex: The customer asked for a refund.
We have many regular customers.
The storm caused serious damage to the roof.
Smoking causes damage to your lungs.
She has been deaf since she was a child.
He is deaf in one ear.

delay.def: A situation where something happens later than planned
ex: There was a two-hour delay at the airport.
Sorry for the delay in replying to your email.

deliver.def: To take something to a person or place
ex: They deliver pizza to your door.
The postman delivered a package this morning.
The workers demanded higher pay.
She demanded an explanation from him.

depend.def: To be affected by or decided by something
ex: The price depends on the size.
You can always depend on her for help.

design.def: To plan and draw how something will be made
ex: She designed a new logo for the company.
Who designed this beautiful building?

destroy.def: To damage something so badly that it cannot be used
ex: The fire destroyed the entire building.
War destroys people's lives.

develop.def: To grow or change into something bigger or better
ex: The town developed quickly over ten years.
She is developing her language skills.
Do you have a digital camera?
We live in a digital world now.

disappear.def: To stop being visible or to stop existing
ex: The cat disappeared behind the wall.
Many old traditions are disappearing.

discuss.def: To talk about something with someone
ex: We need to discuss this problem together.
They discussed the plan over lunch.

double.def: Twice as much or two of something
ex: I would like a double room, please.
The price has doubled in five years.

dust.def: Tiny pieces of dirt that you see on surfaces
ex: There was a thick layer of dust on the shelf.
She cleaned the dust off the table.
She earns a good salary.
He earns money by teaching guitar.

electronic.def: Using electricity and small parts to work
ex: I bought a new electronic device.
Electronic music is very popular now.
She was embarrassed when she forgot his name.
I felt embarrassed about my mistake.

emergency.def: A serious situation that needs fast action
ex: Call 112 in an emergency.
The hospital is ready for any emergency.

emotion.def: A strong feeling such as love, anger, or fear
ex: He finds it hard to show his emotions.
Music can bring out strong emotions.

employ.def: To pay someone to work for you
ex: The company employs over 200 people.
She was employed as a teacher.
The room was completely empty.
Is this seat empty?

encourage.def: To give someone hope or support to do something
ex: The teacher encouraged her students to read more.
His success encouraged me to try harder.
I read the entire book in one day.
The entire class passed the exam.

especially.def: More than usual or more than others
ex: I love all fruit, especially strawberries.
It is cold today, especially in the morning.

eventually.def: In the end, after a long time
ex: She eventually found her keys in her pocket.
He eventually agreed to help us.

exactly.def: In a way that is completely correct
ex: That is exactly what I mean.
The train left at exactly 9 o'clock.
The doctor examined his knee.
Police examined the car for evidence.
The food at that restaurant is excellent.
She did an excellent job on the project.
The children were excited about the trip.
I am so excited to see you again!

excuse.def: A reason you give to explain a mistake or bad behavior
ex: He always has an excuse for being late.
That is not a good excuse.
This bag is too expensive for me.
Living in the city center is very expensive.

explore.def: To travel around a new place to learn about it
ex: We explored the old part of the city.
Children love to explore nature.

extra.def: More than what is normal or expected
ex: Would you like extra cheese on your pizza?
She works extra hours on Fridays.
The heat was extreme last summer.
Extreme sports are dangerous but exciting.

factory.def: A building where things are made by machines
ex: My father works in a car factory.
The factory produces 1000 units a day.

fail.def: To not succeed in doing something
ex: He failed his driving test twice.
The plan failed because of bad weather.

fairly.def: More than a little but not very
ex: The test was fairly easy.
She speaks English fairly well.

familiar.def: Known to you, easy to recognize
ex: His face looks familiar to me.
Are you familiar with this software?

farm.def: A place where food is grown or animals are kept
ex: They live on a small farm in the countryside.
The farm produces milk and cheese.

fault.def: Responsibility for a mistake or problem
ex: It was my fault that we missed the bus.
The accident was not her fault.
The female students organized the event.
There are more female doctors now than before.

fever.def: A body temperature that is higher than normal
ex: She stayed home because she had a fever.
The child had a high fever all night.
The flight to Istanbul takes two hours.
Our flight was delayed by one hour.

flood.def: A large amount of water covering land that is usually dry
ex: The flood destroyed many homes.
Heavy rain caused floods in the south.
I will remember this moment forever.
Nothing lasts forever.

freeze.def: To become very cold and turn to ice
ex: Water freezes at zero degrees.
I am freezing — can you close the window?
I always buy fresh vegetables from the market.
Let me get you some fresh towels.

furniture.def: Tables, chairs, beds, etc. in a room
ex: We need to buy new furniture for the living room.
The furniture in this office is very old.

garage.def: A building where you keep your car
ex: He parked his car in the garage.
The garage door was left open.

gentle.def: Kind, calm, and careful not to hurt
ex: Be gentle with the baby.
She has a gentle voice.
I am glad you could come to the party.
She was glad to hear the good news.
My goal is to speak English fluently.
He scored three goals in one match.

guest.def: A person you invite to your home or to an event
ex: We are having guests for dinner tonight.
The hotel has 200 guest rooms.

guilty.def: Having done something wrong, or feeling bad about it
ex: The jury found him guilty.
I feel guilty about not calling my mother.

habit.def: Something you do regularly, often without thinking
ex: Smoking is a bad habit.
She has a habit of biting her nails.

handle.def: To deal with a situation or problem
ex: She handled the problem very well.
I cannot handle all this work alone.

hang.def: To put something so it is held from above
ex: She hung the picture on the wall.
Hang your coat on the hook.
I can hardly hear you — speak louder.
She hardly ever goes out at night.
There was a heap of dirty clothes on the floor.
He left a heap of papers on his desk.
They live in a huge house.
The concert was a huge success.
Please call me back immediately.
She immediately knew something was wrong.
I want to improve my English.
The weather is improving slowly.

indeed.def: Used to make something stronger or confirm it
ex: It is indeed a difficult problem.
Thank you very much indeed.

instructions.def: Information telling you how to do something
ex: Read the instructions before using the machine.
Follow the instructions on the packet.

intend.def: To plan or mean to do something
ex: I intend to finish this book today.
She intended to call but forgot.

introduce.def: To tell people each other's names when they first meet
ex: Let me introduce you to my friend.
He introduced himself to the new neighbors.

invite.def: To ask someone to come to an event or place
ex: She invited all her friends to the party.
Would you like to invite him for dinner?

island.def: A piece of land with water all around it
ex: We took a boat to the island.
The island has beautiful beaches.

journey.def: The act of traveling from one place to another
ex: The journey took five hours by train.
Have a safe journey!

lawyer.def: A person whose job is to help people with the law
ex: She hired a lawyer to help with the case.
He wants to become a lawyer.

lazy.def: Not wanting to work or be active
ex: He is too lazy to clean his room.
I felt lazy and stayed in bed all morning.

lend.def: To give something to someone for a short time
ex: Can you lend me some money until Friday?
She lent her umbrella to her colleague.

limit.def: The most that is allowed or possible
ex: The speed limit here is 50 km/h.
There is a limit to what I can do.
What a lovely day!
She wore a lovely dress to the wedding.

manage.def: To succeed in doing something difficult
ex: I managed to finish the report on time.
How do you manage to stay so calm?

meal.def: Food that you eat at a particular time of day
ex: Breakfast is the most important meal of the day.
We had a delicious meal at the new restaurant.
She mentioned that she was moving to a new city.
Did he mention the meeting?
Mix the flour and eggs in a bowl.
You should not mix work and personal life.

murder.def: The crime of killing someone on purpose
ex: The police are investigating a murder.
He was arrested for murder.
Her handwriting is very neat.
Keep your desk neat and clean.
She felt nervous before the interview.
I always get nervous before exams.

nowadays.def: At the present time, in contrast to the past
ex: Nowadays, everyone has a smartphone.
People travel more nowadays than before.
It was obvious that she was lying.
The answer is obvious if you think about it.

opportunity.def: A chance to do something good
ex: This job is a great opportunity for you.
Don't miss the opportunity to travel abroad.
It was just an ordinary day.
She is no ordinary student — she is brilliant.

ought.def: Should, used to say what is right to do
ex: You ought to apologize to her.
We ought to leave before it gets dark.

patient.def: Able to wait calmly without getting angry
ex: Please be patient — the doctor will see you soon.
She is very patient with her students.

perform.def: To do something, especially in front of people
ex: The band performed at the festival.
The surgeon performed the operation successfully.
We had a pleasant evening together.
The weather is quite pleasant today.
There is plenty of food for everyone.
We have plenty of time before the train leaves.
He is always polite to strangers.
It is polite to say thank you.

pollution.def: Damage caused to air, water, or land by harmful substances
ex: Air pollution is a big problem in cities.
The factory caused pollution in the river.

pour.def: To make liquid flow from a container
ex: She poured water into the glass.
It was pouring with rain all day.

prefer.def: To like one thing more than another
ex: I prefer tea to coffee.
She prefers to walk rather than drive.

pretend.def: To act as if something is true when it is not
ex: He pretended to be asleep.
Don't pretend you didn't hear me.
Wearing a seatbelt can prevent injuries.
We must prevent this from happening again.

proud.def: Feeling pleased about something you or someone else has done
ex: She is proud of her children.
I am proud to be part of this team.

prove.def: To show that something is true
ex: Can you prove that you were at home?
The test results proved he was right.

publish.def: To make a book, article, or information available to the public
ex: She published her first novel last year.
The results will be published next week.

punish.def: To make someone suffer for doing something wrong
ex: The teacher punished the student for cheating.
He was punished for breaking the rules.

realize.def: To understand something you did not know before
ex: I realized I had left my phone at home.
She suddenly realized the truth.

recommend.def: To suggest that something is good or suitable
ex: I recommend this book — it is great.
The doctor recommended more exercise.

reduce.def: To make something smaller or less
ex: We need to reduce our spending.
The shop reduced prices by 20 percent.
She refused to answer the question.
He refused the offer politely.

regret.def: To feel sorry about something you did or did not do
ex: I regret not studying harder at school.
She regretted her decision immediately.
Please remind me to call the dentist.
This song reminds me of my childhood.

repair.def: To fix something that is broken
ex: He repaired the broken chair.
How much will it cost to repair my phone?

replace.def: To put something new in place of something old
ex: We need to replace the old batteries.
Nobody can replace a good friend.

respect.def: A feeling of admiration for someone
ex: Children should show respect to their elders.
I have great respect for her work.

responsibility.def: A duty to deal with something
ex: It is your responsibility to lock the door.
She took responsibility for the mistake.

rob.def: To steal from a person or place
ex: Someone robbed the bank last night.
He was robbed on his way home.
The sea was very rough during the storm.
The surface of the road is rough.

salary.def: The money you receive every month for your work
ex: His salary is not enough to pay the rent.
She got a salary increase this year.

satisfy.def: To make someone pleased by giving them what they need
ex: The answer did not satisfy her.
Nothing satisfies him — he always wants more.
The loud noise scared the children.
Don't scare me like that!

secret.def: Something that is kept hidden from others
ex: Can you keep a secret?
She told me all her secrets.

select.def: To choose something from a group
ex: Please select your preferred language.
He was selected for the national team.
The children sleep in separate rooms.
Keep your personal life separate from work.
She had a severe headache.
The country faced severe flooding.

shall.def: Used to make suggestions or offers
ex: Shall we go for a walk?
Shall I open the window?

shame.def: A painful feeling caused by doing something wrong
ex: He felt shame after lying to his parents.
What a shame you cannot come to the party.

sharp.def: Having a thin edge that can cut
ex: Be careful — that knife is very sharp.
The cat has sharp claws.
There was silence in the room after he spoke.
She sat in silence for a long time.
Don't ask silly questions.
It was a silly mistake.

smooth.def: Having an even surface without bumps
ex: The baby has very smooth skin.
The road was smooth and easy to drive on.

steal.def: To take something that belongs to someone else
ex: Someone stole my wallet on the bus.
It is wrong to steal.

storm.def: Very bad weather with strong wind and rain
ex: A terrible storm hit the city last night.
The storm knocked down several trees.
She succeeded in passing all her exams.
If you keep trying, you will succeed.

temperature.def: How hot or cold something is
ex: The temperature dropped to minus five last night.
What is the temperature outside?
The theme of the story is love and loss.
The party had a space theme.
She cut the bread into thin slices.
He is tall and thin.

throat.def: The front part of your neck, or the passage inside
ex: I have a sore throat.
The food got stuck in his throat.
These shoes are too tight.
Hold on tight — the bus is about to move.

tongue.def: The soft part inside your mouth that you use for tasting
ex: She burned her tongue on the hot soup.
The word was on the tip of my tongue.

tough.def: Difficult, or strong and not easily broken
ex: It was a tough decision to make.
You need to be tough to survive in this job.

tradition.def: A custom that has existed for a long time
ex: It is a tradition to eat turkey on Thanksgiving.
We should keep our traditions alive.

translate.def: To change words from one language to another
ex: Can you translate this sentence into Turkish?
She translates books for a living.

treat.def: To behave towards someone in a particular way
ex: You should treat people with kindness.
The doctor treated his injury.

trick.def: Something done to fool or surprise someone
ex: The magician showed us an amazing trick.
It was just a trick — nobody was hurt.
The building is ugly but functional.
She said some ugly things during the argument.

unfortunately.def: Used to say that something is sad or disappointing
ex: Unfortunately, the shop was closed.
Unfortunately, I cannot come to your party.
The team united to solve the problem.
Music can unite people from different cultures.
She was upset when she heard the news.
Don't get upset — we can fix this.

valley.def: A low area of land between hills or mountains
ex: The village is in a beautiful green valley.
A river runs through the valley.
The shop sells a wide variety of cheese.
Variety is the spice of life.

warn.def: To tell someone about a possible danger
ex: I warned you not to go there alone.
The sign warns drivers about the sharp turn.

wealth.def: A large amount of money or possessions
ex: His family has great wealth.
Health is more important than wealth.

weigh.def: To measure how heavy something is
ex: How much do you weigh?
She weighed the fruit before paying.
This painting is worth a million dollars.
The film is definitely worth watching.
